item.title: Anthropic in talks to give US government access to its Mythos model
item.description: Discussions come despite federal lawsuits over whether the start-up is a national security risk

item.title: Former EY employee sues firm over dismissal after viral Israel speech
item.description: George Washington University graduate says she was fired after remarks criticising actions in Gaza

item.title: New York City’s super-rich cry foul over proposed tax on second homes
item.description: Billionaire hedge fund managers accuse mayor Zohran Mamdani of ‘demonising philanthropists’

item.title: Netflix founder Reed Hastings to step down from board
item.description: Chair to depart as streamer reports profit boost from $2.8bn termination fee after Warner Bros bidding war

item.title: Boing! Spring time for the markets
item.description: Investors seem delighted, but why?

item.title: US oil bosses warn Trump to stand firm against Iran’s Hormuz toll
item.description: Executives step up lobbying to convince White House that Tehran controlling the strategic waterway sets dangerous precedent

item.title: Reeves vows to cut link between gas and electricity prices in UK
item.description: Chancellor says she will look at ways of ensuring the wholesale price of electricity is set more often by renewables

item.title: Did news of EQT’s takeover approach for Intertek leak 7,000 miles away?
item.description: ¡Crudo picante!

item.title: OnlyFans-style age checks are the standard, says Starmer
item.description: Prime minister says ‘things can’t go on like this’ in meeting with social media companies

item.title: UK firms ‘should be worried’ about Anthropic’s latest AI model, minister says
item.description: Kanishka Narayan says Britain needs to ‘make most of opportunities’ as government launches £500mn unit

item.title: Schroders shareholders approve £9.9bn sale to Nuveen
item.description: Two centuries of family ownership of the British financial institution will end

item.title: The Mythos cyber scare signals the economics of AI scarcity
item.description: As the capabilities of frontier models advance, gaining access to the technology could become critically important

item.title: Monte dei Paschi drama has no heroes, only winners
item.description: Luigi Lovaglio’s survival at the world’s oldest bank is down to typical Italian wheeling and dealing

item.title: AI has an Awful Image problem
item.description: Modern-day Luddites are gaining ground because tech titans haven’t shown people how innovation will improve their lives

item.title: UK to scrap extra carbon charge paid by fossil fuel power stations
item.description: Move broadly welcomed by industry comes as cost concerns rise up political agenda

item.title: Kering CEO De Meo aims to double profitability in turnaround push
item.description: New chief says luxury group is at a ‘turning point’ as he lays out plans to boost profit and reduce dependence on Gucci

item.title: Getty must sell Shutterstock editorial business, says UK antitrust watchdog
item.description: CMA warns that $3.7bn merger of photo agencies depends on sale to address competition concerns

item.title: Russia, Ukraine and US named in €580mn legal battle over Nord Stream blasts
item.description: London High Court weighs case over who should pay for damage to Russia-Europe pipes sabotaged in 2022

item.title: Autoglass owner Belron prepares €30bn Amsterdam IPO
item.description: Listing for world’s largest car glass repair group would boost Europe’s lagging market

item.title: Crypto and AI Pacs raise $250mn ahead of US midterm elections
item.description: Investors Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz gave $25mn to pro-AI Super Pac in first quarter of year

item.title: UK testing specialist Intertek rejects EQT takeover proposal
item.description: Swedish private equity firm’s non-binding approach rebuffed as shares in FTSE 100 group surge

item.title: SNP pledges ‘1970s-style’ food price cap
item.description: Pledge by Scotland’s main pro-independence party could provoke constitutional row with Westminster

item.title: Watch live: how big are the risks in private credit?
item.description: Take part as FT journalists answer your questions in a subscriber webinar

item.title: Premier League clubs rack up almost £800mn of losses
item.description: Club accounts for 2024-25 season show cumulative losses ballooned as teams spent heavily on players

item.title: The costly voting structure for US funds needs an overhaul
item.description: The current outdated system was designed for a very different era

item.title: How (un)reliable are AI agents?
item.description: Consistency matters more than average accuracy in safety-critical domains

item.title: British ship financier charged by NCA over Russian sanction violations
item.description: John Ormerod accused of making money transfers after being designated for arranging oil tanker purchases for Lukoil

item.title: Appetite for PepsiCo’s cheaper snacks boosts foods group
item.description: Company is focusing on price cuts and more products with natural ingredients

item.title: How to finance geothermal energy
item.description: Zanskar Geothermal secures a $40mn development capital facility that could serve as a model for other developers

item.title: UK rollout of Covid-19 vaccines was ‘extraordinary feat’, inquiry says
item.description: Report also calls for ministers to make ‘urgent’ reforms to compensation scheme for people who ‘suffered harm’
